programming-fundamentals.description: Master core programming concepts, variables, control flow, functions, and elementary data handling.
programming-fundamentals.whyImportant: It forms the foundation of all software development. You cannot build complex systems without mastering loops, variables, and functional logic.

data-structures-algorithms.description: Master memory structures, sorting/searching algorithms, complexity math, and optimization techniques.
data-structures-algorithms.whyImportant: DSA is key to writing high-performance code and is the primary subject evaluated in coding rounds at top tech companies.

sql-databases.description: Understand database models, normalization theory, indexing, transactional queries, and query performance scaling.
sql-databases.whyImportant: Almost all systems require persistent storage. Design relational schemas and writing performant SQL query strings are fundamental backend skills.

operating-systems.description: Learn memory management, thread scheduling, file systems, caching, and kernel operations.
operating-systems.whyImportant: Understanding the OS allows you to write concurrent systems, manage system resource limits, and debug concurrency deadlocks.

computer-networks.description: Understand communication protocols, TCP/IP layouts, routing strategies, DNS operations, and network sockets.
computer-networks.whyImportant: Required to design distributed architectures, coordinate API configurations, and troubleshoot connection latencies.

object-oriented-programming.description: Master encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction, interface models, and SOLID design patterns.
object-oriented-programming.whyImportant: OOP is the dominant design paradigm in backend software development, enabling code reusability and structured components.

system-design.description: Learn to architect scalable distributed systems, load balancing, sharding, caching, microservices, and message brokers.
system-design.whyImportant: Required for designing systems that handle millions of requests, optimizing cloud budgets, and qualifying for senior developer roles.

software-projects.description: Assemble and deploy robust software systems using clean architectures, testing pipelines, and environment setups.
software-projects.whyImportant: Applying knowledge to realistic project builds demonstrates execution capability and builds your public portfolio.

interview-prep.description: Prepare for technical reviews, coding exercises, resume reviews, and HR behavioral screenings.
interview-prep.whyImportant: Translates technical skill into job offers. Covers standard resume guidelines, STAR methods, and coding boards.

circuit-analysis.description: Master circuit theorems, Kirchhoff's laws, AC/DC analysis, and transient response calculations.
circuit-analysis.whyImportant: It forms the core foundation of all electrical engineering. Essential for analyzing and designing basic analog networks.

digital-logic-design.description: Learn logic gates, combinational networks, sequential circuits, flip-flops, and FPGA architectures.
digital-logic-design.whyImportant: Essential to design digital circuits, microchips, CPU architectures, and program logic controllers.

electrical-machines.description: Understand transformers, DC motors, synchronous machines, and induction motors operating models.
electrical-machines.whyImportant: Required to design power plants, factory motors, generators, and work in electricity boards or PSUs.

power-systems-analysis.description: Understand generation, transmission lines, load flows, power distribution, and grid protections.
power-systems-analysis.whyImportant: Crucial for working in electrical utilities, power grids, PSUs, and public engineering posts.

control-systems.description: Learn PID controllers, feedback systems, transfer functions, and frequency stability tests.
control-systems.whyImportant: Key to industrial automation, robotics, auto-pilots, and manufacturing systems.

pcb-design-kicad.description: Learn schematic design, trace routing, footprints placement, high-speed layout rules, and Gerber generation.
pcb-design-kicad.whyImportant: Required to build actual electronics products, physical microcontrollers boards, and work in hardware departments.

engineering-mathematics.description: Master linear algebra, calculus, differential equations, and numerical analysis.
engineering-mathematics.whyImportant: The core mathematical background tested in all public engineering exams (GATE, state PSC, etc.).

general-aptitude-reasoning.description: Prepare for quantitative aptitude, logical reasoning, verbal competency, and speed arithmetic.
general-aptitude-reasoning.whyImportant: The screening filter section for almost all placement drives and government competitive examinations.

general-studies-gk.description: Explore history, geography, constitution rules, current events, and economic guidelines.
general-studies-gk.whyImportant: Crucial scoring sections in administrative services and state electricity boards examinations.

government-exam-projects.description: Perform structural calculations, substation designs, and program CPU process simulators.
government-exam-projects.whyImportant: Validates your design engineering understanding before entering public workshops.
topics: Concrete wall mount casing structural specs
